toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect crash dumps if any test crashes the host. 

For .NET, crash dump collection can be configured via environment variables for the .NET runtime's crash dump generation. The key environment variables are:

- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Values: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump


- Alternatively, since this project uses the Microsoft Testing Platform (indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` being true), I can use the `--blame-crash` option with `dotnet test`, which automatically collects crash dumps when tests fail.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-b5cd3244f25c4c6e8980f2a95611e56a && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command used:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a crash dump (`.dmp` file) if the test host process crashes unexpectedly. Dumps are written to a `TestResults` directory alongside sequence/blame files for diagnosis. Since no crash occurred here, no dump was generated.
